summary: Iteration 8 (Student Home promo+icons, Leaderboard single-line headers, Mock Interviews calendar DatePicker + has_available_slots default, /jobs city-synonym + partial skill regex, Profile Sign Out with confirm). Added /app/backend/tests/test_iteration8.py with 19 NEW iter-8 tests. test_iteration8.py: 19/19 GREEN. Full backend suite: 135/136 GREEN — single failure is the pre-existing test_iteration4::TestLeaderboardIteration4::test_leaderboard_filters caused by accumulated seed data (1302 students; 123 match location=Bangalore&skill=Python&category=fresher; page_size capped at 100; freshly-created student with composite_score=0 lands at rank ~123 off page 1). Not a regression from iter-8. Backend coverage end-to-end verified: GET /api/professionals as student defaults has_available_slots=true and ONLY returns pros with at least one future available slot (pro with no slots is hidden; pro with future slot is included; has_available_slots=false includes all); skill filter is partial+case-insensitive over expertise[] AND skills[] ('java' matches 'Core Java'); location filter is partial+case-insensitive over current_location; category filter splits fresher (experience_years<=0) vs experienced; date filter (YYYY-MM-DD) matches only slots on that day. GET /api/jobs?location=Bangalore returns jobs located in 'Bengaluru' via CITY_SYNONYMS (and Mumbai↔Bombay, Chennai↔Madras, Kolkata↔Calcutta, Gurgaon↔Gurugram, Trivandrum↔Thiruvananthapuram all verified parametrized). GET /api/jobs?skill=java returns 'Core Java' + 'Java Full Stack' (partial regex case-insensitive) and correctly excludes 'Python' jobs. GET /api/jobs?skill=sql returns 'PL/SQL' + 'Oracle SQL'. Frontend smoke (mobile viewport 390x844, web preview): /student/dashboard hero renders 'Your Resume Score 0/100' + 'Improve your Resume Score by attending Mock Interviews' + Book Mock Interview card with user-tie icon + Apply for Referral card with handshake icon (legacy 'Earn credits by referrals & interviews' copy removed). /student/mock-interviews: DatePickerField opens modal listing weekday-prefixed dates (Mon Jun 8 2026, Tue Jun 9 2026, Wed Jun 10 2026, ...) with no manual entry — confirmed visually. Skill filter input remains typeable. /student/leaderboard: Stat component (Score/Interviews/Rating/Applied/Referrals) is implemented with numberOfLines=1 + adjustsFontSizeToFit + minimumFontScale=0.7 + fontSize 11/13 in /app/frontend/app/student/leaderboard.tsx:159-178 — verified in code; runtime verification deferred (leaderboard renders data via AsyncStorage-backed session; my injected localStorage token bypasses AsyncStorage and the page silently catches the unauthenticated API call). /student/profile: sign-out-btn testID present at bottom (outline-red Sign Out button). /professional/profile: logout button uses 'Logout' label (not 'Sign Out' per student parity) and is missing testID. HIGH PRIORITY FRONTEND BUG: Sign Out tap is non-functional on the WEB preview — React Native Web's Alert.alert() with the 3-button form ([Cancel, Yes Sign Out] + an undefined message body) does NOT render any dialog on web (no native confirm/no Modal) — clicking Sign Out does nothing visible and session stays. On native iOS/Android this works. Fix recommendation: replace Alert.alert with a custom Modal (same pattern as DatePickerField) OR fall back to window.confirm on Platform.OS==='web'. Same applies to /professional/profile Logout. Mock OTP still rendered in OTP screen (carry-over from iter-6). RN-Web shadow*/pointerEvents warnings carry over.

minor.issue: page_size hard-capped at 100 (Query(le=100)) — when filtered set >100 a freshly-created test student with composite_score=0 falls off page 1; test_iteration4::test_leaderboard_filters now fails due to seed data accumulation (1302 students, 123 match the test filter). Not a regression from iter-8; consider relaxing the cap OR re-writing the test to bump composite_score (apply to a job before asserting).

When has_available_slots=true AND skill is provided, the slot.skill_set check is intentionally non-strict (line 991-995 in server.py: 'still allow match via pro expertise, so don't strictly exclude'). This is per design (so the broader expertise[] match wins) but worth documenting — a pro with skill 'Java' in expertise but a slot with skill_set ['Python'] will still appear under ?skill=java.
Carried over from iter-6/7: SendGrid 401 in env; mock_otp fallback keeps tests alive but real emails are dropped silently.

ui_bugs.issue: Sign Out / Logout tap is NO-OP on web preview. Alert.alert(title, undefined, [Cancel, Yes Sign Out]) is not rendered by react-native-web for the 3-button form; no dialog, no navigation, no session clear. Reproduce: tap Sign Out → nothing visible → token + user remain in storage. Fix: replace with a custom Modal-based confirmation (already used elsewhere e.g. DatePickerField) OR a Platform.OS==='web' fallback to window.confirm('Are you sure you want to sign out?').

Pro profile Button uses title='Logout' instead of 'Sign Out' — copy mismatch with student profile (spec said 'Pro profile logout also wrapped with the same confirmation', implying same UX/copy). Also missing testID='sign-out-btn' for testability parity.
Mock OTP code still rendered directly on the verify screen whenever response.mock_otp is present. Should be gated on __DEV__ / EXPO_PUBLIC_SHOW_MOCK_OTP.

issues: Carried over: 'shadow*' style props deprecated (use boxShadow); 'props.pointerEvents' deprecated (move into style). Non-blocking RN-Web warnings on every screen load.

react_native_issues.issue: react-native-web does NOT render Alert.alert when the buttons array has >2 entries (or includes a 'destructive' style) — silently no-ops. This is a known RN-Web limitation. Use a custom Modal or Platform-specific shim. Reference: /app/frontend/app/student/profile.tsx:192, /app/frontend/app/professional/profile.tsx:144.

action_items: (HIGH) Fix Sign Out / Logout on web preview — Alert.alert 3-button form is no-op on react-native-web. Replace with a small Modal (Confirm/Cancel) component OR `Platform.OS==='web' ? window.confirm(...) ? doLogout() : null : Alert.alert(...)`. Affects both /student/profile.tsx and /professional/profile.tsx.
(LOW) /professional/profile.tsx: change Button title='Logout' → 'Sign Out' for parity with student spec, and add testID='sign-out-btn'.
(MEDIUM, carry-over) Gate mock_otp UI rendering behind __DEV__ / EXPO_PUBLIC_SHOW_MOCK_OTP.

(LOW) Tighten test_iteration4::test_leaderboard_filters to bump the test student's composite_score (apply to a seeded job) so it stays on page 1 even with growing seed data. Alternatively bump page_size cap in /api/leaderboard/students from 100 to 500.
(LOW, carry-over) Replace shadow* style props with boxShadow; move pointerEvents into style across components.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2259 lines — splitting into routers (auth, profile, jobs, interviews, applications, leaderboard, wallet, admin, payouts, pro_extras) is overdue.

list_professionals (server.py:948) loads up to 500 pros + 5000 slots into memory then python-filters — for a growing user base this should be moved into an aggregation pipeline ($lookup interview_slots on pro_id, $match status:available + start_at:{$gt:now}, then category/skill regex). Currently OK because seed data is small.

list_professionals slot-skill check at line 991-995 has a no-op `pass` branch — confusing dead code; either implement strict slot-skill filter or remove the if-block. The comment 'still allow match via pro expertise, so don't strictly exclude' is correct logic but the if-block adds no behavior.

expand_city (server.py:66) returns [key] (single-element list) for cities not in CITY_SYNONYMS — that's correct, but worth a unit test to guard against accidental empty list which would make q['location'] regex '' (matches everything).
Both profile screens use Alert.alert for confirmation which is broken on web — abstract into a shared `useConfirmDialog` hook that returns Platform-aware behavior so this footgun doesn't recur.
leaderboard pagination cap (page_size le=100) is too tight for a system with 1300+ students and noisy filters; raise to 500 or 1000 and rely on filters for UX.

success_rate.backend: 99.3% (135/136). New iter-8 file 19/19 GREEN. Single failure (test_iteration4::TestLeaderboardIteration4::test_leaderboard_filters) is pre-existing seed-data flakiness, NOT a regression from iter-8.
success_rate.frontend: 75% — verified: dashboard hero+promo+icons, Mock Interviews calendar DatePickerField modal, skill input typeable, Sign Out button rendered at bottom of student profile, /professional/profile credits pill copy 'Available Credits' applied (iter-7 fix carried in). FAILING: Sign Out tap (web preview) is no-op on both profiles due to RN-Web Alert.alert 3-button limitation. NOT verified at runtime (deferred to code review): leaderboard single-line stat-row rendering with real entries (code is correct: numberOfLines=1+adjustsFontSizeToFit+minimumFontScale=0.7+fontSize 11/13 confirmed in source).

seed_data_creation: Throwaway @referme.io pros/students + 1 @acmecorp.io pro created via /auth/signup + /auth/verify-otp during the test run. Multiple TEST jobs posted by the demo-employer fixture for /api/jobs synonym/skill tests. All entries are uniquely-suffixed and identifiable for cleanup if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-8 backend contract verified end-to-end via /app/backend/tests/test_iteration8.py (19 tests). Helper note: interview_slots.create requires duration >= 60 minutes (server-side validation). All 7 CITY_SYNONYMS pairs covered by parametrized test (Bangalore/Bengaluru, Mumbai/Bombay, Chennai/Madras, Kolkata/Calcutta, Gurgaon/Gurugram, Trivandrum/Thiruvananthapuram — plus the bidirectional Bombay→Mumbai and Bengaluru→Bangalore in test_location_*). One PRE-EXISTING test (test_iteration4::test_leaderboard_filters) is now flaky because seed-data has grown — 123 students match its filter and page_size=100 leaves the freshly-created student off page 1. Frontend HIGH priority bug: Sign Out is no-op on web preview because Alert.alert 3-button form is unsupported by react-native-web (silently swallows). On native iOS/Android the flow works; main agent should refactor both /student/profile.tsx:logout and /professional/profile.tsx:logout to use a Modal-based confirmation (same pattern as DatePickerField in /app/frontend/src/components/DateTimePicker.tsx). After fix, retest by tapping data-testid='sign-out-btn' on /student/profile (web preview) and confirming a visible confirm UI then 'Yes, Sign Out' → /welcome + session cleared. AsyncStorage on web uses IndexedDB-backed AsyncStorage; injecting localStorage directly does NOT log the user in — testing must drive through the actual UI (signup → OTP → verify) or use the storage util's setItem from app context.
rca_of_issue: Sign-out web-preview no-op RCA: React Native Web's Alert.alert implementation maps to window.alert (1 button) or window.confirm (2 buttons OK/Cancel). When the buttons array contains 3+ entries or any entry with style:'destructive', react-native-web does NOT render any web-native dialog and the call becomes a silent no-op (no error thrown). Both /app/frontend/app/student/profile.tsx:192-206 and /app/frontend/app/professional/profile.tsx:144-158 invoke Alert.alert with an undefined message body and a buttons=[Cancel, Yes Sign Out] array where the second button has style:'destructive' — exactly the unsupported variant. Reproduction: open https://refer-connect-11.preview.emergentagent.com/student/profile while logged in as a student → scroll to bottom → tap the red-outline 'Sign Out' button → nothing visible, no navigation, AsyncStorage rm_token still present. Mitigation: refactor to a custom <ConfirmModal /> component (RN-Web compatible) used by both screens, OR add a Platform.OS==='web' guard that falls back to window.confirm() (limited to 2 buttons). Leaderboard test failure RCA: test_iteration4::test_leaderboard_filters creates a brand-new student (composite_score=0) then asserts they appear in /api/leaderboard/students?location=Bangalore&skill=Python&category=fresher&page_size=100. Current db.users has 1302 students; 123 match those exact filters; the new student is sorted to rank ~123 by composite_score desc and falls beyond the page_size=100 cap (Query enforces le=100). Mitigation: bump the test student's composite_score before asserting (e.g. apply to a seeded job → jobs_applied=1 → composite_score += 2) OR raise the leaderboard endpoint's page_size cap in server.py:1901 from le=100 to le=500.
